title: Hieracium terekianum
intro: Hieracium terekianum, also known as Terek hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is a perennial plant used in traditional medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antitumor properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits in treating various conditions such as digestive issues, wounds, and certain types of cancer. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium terekianum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Hieracium terekianum include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
taxonomy: Hieracium terekianum is classified within the plant kingdom as a species of flowering plant. More specifically, it belongs to the family Asteraceae. This family is commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. Hieracium terekianum is further classified as a member of the genus Hieracium, which includes over 1000 species of hawkweeds. It is native to certain mountainous regions in the Caucasus.
